items.description: La Roja's patient possession game meets the Albiceleste's trademark resilience in the World Cup final in New York on Sunday. items.title: Russia hits Kyiv with ballistic missiles
items.description: Russia has launched one of its largest ballistic missile attacks of the conflict in Ukraine. At least one person was killed and 16 others were injured after around two dozen ballistic missiles struck Kyiv overnight. Witnesses described charred apartment buildings with windows blown out, mangled debris, and overturned cars scattered across the city. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said six districts of the capital were hit. France 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports from Kyiv.

items.title: Kuwait, Bahrain respond to Iranian attacks amid growing escalation
items.description: The risk of a wider war in the Middle East is growing after authorities in Bahrain and Kuwait said they intercepted missiles and drones launched from Iran. The escalation comes after the US Embassy in Jordan urged American citizens, in a social media post, to avoid Aqaba's airport and seaport because of a specific and credible threat. items.title: Cuban dissident artist arrives in US after 5-years in prison
items.description: A hero's welcome for Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara as he arrived in Miami following his release from prison in Cuba. The dissident artist accepted exile after the United States granted him humanitarian parole. Otero Alcántara co-founded the San Isidro Movement, a collective of Havana-based artists, writers, and musicians that protests against the Cuban government. In 2021, he was arrested in Havana during anti-government demonstrations and was later sentenced to five years in prison on charges of public disorder, contempt, and disrespect for national symbols. Carla Colomé Santiago, a Cuban journalist based in Miami, shares her insights.

items.title: When the wells run dry, By Wole Olaoye
items.description: I like to think that I’m a citizen of the world. Truthfully, I have not been raised to have racial or ethnic boundaries, even as I’ve been socialised to appreciate the beauty of my culture and the refreshing diversity observable among humankind. items.title: Spain-Argentina: La Roja claim second World Cup title in extra time
items.description: Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey to win the World Cup, completing a historic Euro-World Cup double. Ferran Torres' late strike sealed victory for a dominant Spanish side against a 10-man Argentina, ending Lionel Messi's World Cup career on a bittersweet note. Relive our liveblog for minute-by-minute commentary.
